candidate.name: Promote pitch-bounded extreme traction recovery
candidate.hypothesis: The prior 2.0 traction recovery hip scale crosses a sagittal pitch-divergence boundary during severe slip. Latching severity from measured forward velocity or a 0.2 rad backward-pitch excursion, then bounding only the severe response at 1.72, should preserve mild-slip traction authority while preventing backward tumble.
candidate.expectedEffect: Eliminate all ten extreme-traction baseline violations, retain every completed regression gate, and keep friction 0.05 as honest non-gating stress evidence.
